---
title: "Completed Fertility in the United States"
subtitle: "Children ever born, by age, year, and birth cohort"
date: last-modified
resources:
  - fertility-visualizer.html
---

Placeholder intro. The chart below is interactive: switch the x-axis between
age, year, and birth cohort in the sidebar, toggle the three-year smoothing, and
select which series to show.

::: {.column-page}

```{=html}
<!--
  The visualizer is embedded as an iframe so its Tailwind/React bundle stays
  isolated from this page's Bootstrap styling.

  height: 720px works for desktop (sidebar + chart side by side). Below 768px
  the layout stacks vertically and may want more height; bump it if it clips.
  Swap .column-page for .column-screen-inset above if you want it near-full-width.
-->
<iframe
  src="fertility-visualizer.html"
  title="Completed Fertility Visualizer"
  loading="lazy"
  style="width: 100%; height: 720px; border: 0; border-radius: 12px;">
</iframe>
```

:::

Source: CPS Fertility Supplement via IPUMS (Version 13.0). Completed fertility is
the weighted (FRSUPPWT) mean of children ever born (FREVER), by year and age.
Calculations by Mike Konczal.
</content>
</invoke>
